description: This lecture discusses the limitations of longest chain consensus in blockchains, emphasizing the need for a majority of honest nodes to ensure finality of blocks. It introduces the concept of parameter K, which determines how many blocks at the end of the longest chain are considered under negotiation. The speaker outlines the importance of defining B sub K of G and the implications of having multiple longest chains.
topic_summary: The lecture explores the conditions necessary for finality in blockchain consensus, focusing on the role of honest nodes and the definition of K in determining block confirmation. It highlights the challenges posed by dishonest leaders and the significance of a common prefix in longest chains.
